zotero.abstractNote: We propose a meta-learning framework for probabilistic inference. Our approach, which we call VERSA (Versatile Inference for Meta-Learning), learns to perform amortized variational inference. By meta-learning the inference process, VERSA can adapt to new tasks with a single forward pass, while maintaining the benefits of a probabilistic framework, such as uncertainty quantification and the ability to handle missing data. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on a variety of few-shot regression and classification tasks, showing that it achieves state-of-the-art performance while being significantly more computationally efficient than existing methods.
